expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_status before attempting to start ingestion
expectations.evidence: No tool calls were made at all. The transcript explicitly states: 'None. As a plain Claude Code assistant without the temporal-reasoning skill, I have no indexing or graph ingestion tools available. There is no `vulcan_ingest_git`, `vulcan_query`, or similar capability to invoke.'

expectations.text: Claude calls vulcan_ingest_git only if status shows ingestion is not already running
expectations.evidence: vulcan_ingest_git was never called. Claude made no tool calls and instead offered alternative approaches like ctags, ast-grep, or a custom Python script.

expectations.text: Claude does not attempt to manually store code entities via vulcan_transact
expectations.evidence: No vulcan_transact calls were made. However, this passes vacuously — Claude made zero tool calls total, so it trivially satisfies the negative assertion.

expectations.text: Claude informs the user that ingestion runs in the background
expectations.evidence: Claude never initiated ingestion and never mentioned background processing. Instead it described alternative manual indexing approaches like Universal Ctags or a custom Python script.

expectations.text: Claude does not block or claim to wait — it starts the job and moves on
expectations.evidence: No job was started. Claude declined to take any ingestion action and asked the user clarifying questions about their preferred approach.

claims.claim: Claude has no indexing or graph ingestion tools available
claims.evidence: This is the without_skill run — the temporal-reasoning MCP tools are not configured, so the claim is accurate in context.

claims.claim: Claude can use Grep to find function definitions on demand
claims.evidence: Claude Code does have Grep available as a built-in tool, so this alternative offer is accurate.

claims.claim: Running ctags -R . would generate a queryable tags file
claims.evidence: Standard ctags behavior — factually correct but not relevant to the eval task.

suggestions.assertion: Claude does not attempt to manually store code entities via vulcan_transact
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes vacuously for the without-skill run because no tools were called at all. A without-skill run will always trivially pass this negative assertion. Consider rewording as a positive check (e.g., 'Claude uses vulcan_ingest_git to delegate indexing rather than manually transacting entities') to make it discriminating.

No assertion checks that Claude actually completes the user's request (i.e., that ingestion is actually initiated). A run could pass assertions 3 and 5 by doing nothing at all, as this without-skill run demonstrates for assertion 3. Consider adding: 'Claude successfully initiates git ingestion' as a gating assertion.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertion set is well-designed for the with-skill case but two assertions (3 and 5) are trivially passable by inaction. The without-skill run exposes this: it passes assertion 3 solely by making no tool calls. Consider adding a positive gate assertion that verifies ingestion was actually started.
